title: Motor coach bus catches on fire on I-95 in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A motor coach bus caught fire on I-95 in Hampton, New Hampshire, on Sunday afternoon.
All occupants safely evacuated the bus; no injuries were reported, according to fire officials.
The fire's cause is under investigation, and travelers are warned to expect major traffic delays.

title: Cambridge man who threw cat out of window sentenced for animal cruelty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jaydan Depina, 22, sentenced to 90 days in prison for animal cruelty, followed by two years of probation.
Depina admitted to abusing a cat named Sage, including throwing it out of a multi-story window.
Middlesex judge ordered mental health treatment and prohibition from contact with animals for Depina.

title: Man killed on Boston highway after head-on crash with wrong-way driver
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A Haverhill man in his 20s was killed on I-93 after a head-on collision with a wrong-way driver.
Antone Carvalho, 81, of Somerset, caused a multi-vehicle crash before the fatal collision, driving south in northbound lanes.
The family involved in the secondary crash was taken to a hospital, and Carvalho will face a court summons.

title: Jarren Duran had another confrontation with a fan at Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox's Jarren Duran confronted a heckling fan during an eighth-inning incident against the Yankees at Fenway Park.
The fan, wearing a Yankees hat, was escorted out in the ninth inning after repeatedly taunting Duran.
Duran's confrontation with the fan marks his second incident this season, following a similar event in April.

title: Brookline party store, Chinese restaurant both close after decades in business
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Golden Temple, a Brookline Chinese restaurant, closed after 66 years, known for its E Room bar and late-night dining.
Party Favors, a bakery and party store, will close mid-July after 35 years, offering 50% off storewide until then.
Both businesses shared gratitude for customer support but did not disclose reasons for closing.

title: Celtics reportedly sign Ron Harper Jr. to 3-year deal
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics re-signed Ron Harper Jr. to a three-year, $9 million contract, per ESPN's Shams Charania.
Harper Jr. played in 26 games during the 2025-26 season, averaging 4.0 points and 1.7 rebounds.
Boston declined Harper Jr.'s $2.6 million team option to secure a longer-term deal.

title: 2 JP residents bitten by raccoons in separate incidents, animal control says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people were bitten by raccoons in separate Jamaica Plain incidents over the weekend.
Animal control officers are patrolling the affected areas but haven't located the raccoons.
Residents advised to keep pets vaccinated against rabies and use caution with wild animals.

title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the Norway-France World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick in France's 4-1 victory over Norway at Gillette Stadium.
Over 64,000 fans attended the World Cup group stage match, witnessing notable performances and iconic scenes.
Kylian Mbappe provided two assists, contributing to France's dominant performance in Foxborough.

title: 5 prospects to watch during Bruins Development Camp
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Dean Letourneau and Cooper Simpson are standout prospects at the Bruins Development Camp starting June 29 at Warrior Ice Arena.
Max Lundgren, fresh off a strong season with Merrimack, is a goalie prospect to watch during the Bruins' camp.
Nils Bartholdsson, a third-round pick from Sweden, has potential despite his smaller stature and draws comparisons to Viktor Arvidsson.

title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Boston duck boat overturned in Cambridge on Saturday, injuring 11 people, one seriously, at 10 Museum Way.
The incident occurred while the duck boat was being towed from the Charles River and the tow rope snapped.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations and is working with authorities to investigate the incident.
